subject: VPN drops when switching from Ethernet to Wi-Fi
description:
Every time I undock my laptop and move to Wi-Fi, the VPN disconnects and I have to reconnect manually. Started after last Tuesday's Windows update. Using GlobalProtect v3.2 on the NYC office Wi-Fi, Floor 4.

I've been dealing with this issue for a while now and it's really frustrating. Let me give you more context about what's happening. When I'm connected to the Ethernet via my docking station at my desk, everything works fine. The VPN stays connected, I can access all internal resources, and my connection is stable. But the moment I undock my laptop — literally the second I pull the Ethernet cable or take it off the dock — the VPN drops immediately.

I then have to manually reconnect to the Wi-Fi network (ContosoWiFi-5G on Floor 4), wait for it to authenticate, and then manually launch GlobalProtect again and reconnect to the VPN. The whole process takes about 2-3 minutes each time, and I switch between docked and undocked probably 6-8 times per day because I go to meetings, work from the cafe area, etc.

Here's what I've tried so far:
1. Restarting my laptop - didn't help
2. Forgetting and re-adding the Wi-Fi network - didn't help
3. Updating GlobalProtect client - it says v3.2 is the latest available
4. Checking with colleagues on the same floor - at least 3 others have the same issue
5. Tried using the 2.4GHz network instead of 5GHz - same problem

Some additional technical details:
- Laptop: Dell Latitude 5540, Windows 11 23H2
- NIC: Intel AX211 Wi-Fi 6E
- GlobalProtect version: 3.2.1-build4829
- VPN Gateway: vpn-east.contoso.com
- Last Windows Update: KB5034765 installed on March 12, 2026
- Docking Station: Dell WD22TB4 Thunderbolt

I noticed in the Event Viewer there are some warnings from the WLAN-Autoconfig service that say 'The wireless security for interface Wi-Fi was not started because of an internal failure' around the time of each disconnect. I can share the full event log if needed.

This is really impacting my productivity because I'm in back-to-back meetings most of the afternoon and need to maintain my connection to the internal dashboards and trading platforms. Yesterday I missed an important notification because my VPN was down for about 5 minutes between the dock and the conference room.

Please let me know if you need any more information. I'm available anytime to test or provide remote access.

Best regards,
Sarah Chen
Engineering Department
ext. 4829

---

Disclaimer: This email and any attachments are confidential and intended solely for the use of the individual or entity to whom it is addressed. If you have received this communication in error, please notify the sender immediately and delete the message. Any unauthorized use, disclosure, or distribution is prohibited. Contoso Financial Services, 200 Park Avenue, New York, NY 10166.

---

Sent from my Dell laptop
Contoso Financial Services - Think Forward

P.S. I also want to mention that the same thing happened to my colleague James in Trading last week, and he was told it was a 'known issue' but nobody followed up. Can you please check if there's already a fix being worked on?

P.P.S. One more thing - when the VPN drops and I reconnect, it sometimes puts me on a different VPN gateway (vpn-west.contoso.com instead of vpn-east.contoso.com) and then some internal sites don't load properly until I disconnect and reconnect to force it back to the east gateway.

subject: Calendar sync issue
description:
Hi IT Support,

 

My Outlook calendar is showing duplicate entries for all meetings from last week. Every single meeting appears twice, and when I delete one copy, both disappear. I've tried the following:

  • Restarting Outlook
  • Clearing the offline data file (.ost)
  • Removing and re-adding my Exchange account

The issue started after I accepted a recurring meeting series from Jennifer Wu on Monday. Could that be related?

 

I'm on Outlook Version 2404 (Build 17531.20140) on Windows 11.

 

Marcus Rodriguez
Senior Portfolio Manager | Wealth Management
marcus.rodriguez@contoso.com
Contoso Financial Services
Direct: +1 (212) 555-0147 | Mobile: +1 (917) 555-0283

subject: Database connection timeout
description:
Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout

Sorry, my keyboard was stuck. Here's the actual issue:

The production analytics database (analytics-prod-east.database.windows.net) is returning connection timeouts intermittently. Started around 8:30 AM today. About 40% of queries are failing with:

"A connection was successfully established with the server, but then an error occurred during the pre-login handshake. (provider: TCP Provider, error: 0 - An existing connection was forcibly closed by the remote host.)"

Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout

Apologies, it happened again. Anyway, the remaining 60% of queries complete normally but are 3-4x slower than usual (p95 went from ~200ms to ~800ms). This is affecting the real-time trading dashboard and approximately 150 traders across all three offices are seeing stale data.

Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout Database connection timeout

I really need a new keyboard. The connection pool is configured for max 100 connections and Azure Portal shows the DTU utilization is at 89% which is unusual for this time of day.

subject: Problème avec le logiciel de trading
description:
Bonjour l'équipe IT,

J'ai un problème avec le Bloomberg terminal depuis ce matin. L'application se fige complètement quand j'essaie d'ouvrir plusieurs charts en même temps. Normalement je peux en ouvrir 8-10 sans problème, mais maintenant ça plante après le 3ème.

I'm writing in English now because I realize the portal might not support French. Sorry about that.

The Bloomberg Terminal application freezes when opening more than 3 charts simultaneously. This started this morning after a Windows update was applied overnight. I'm on the London trading floor and my colleagues here (Pierre, Amélie, and François) are experiencing the same issue.

Pouvez-vous vérifier si la mise à jour a causé un conflit? Nous avons besoin de cela pour le marché européen qui ouvre dans 30 minutes.

Merci beaucoup,
Jean-Pierre

P.S. Les logs sont dans C:\Users\jpduval\AppData\Local\Bloomberg\logs\crash_2026-03-19.dmp

subject: Application error - stack trace attached
description:
The risk calculation service is throwing errors. Here's the full stack trace:

java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space
	at java.base/java.util.Arrays.copyOf(Arrays.java:3512)
	at java.base/java.util.ArrayList.grow(ArrayList.java:237)
	at java.base/java.util.ArrayList.grow(ArrayList.java:244)
	at java.base/java.util.ArrayList.add(ArrayList.java:454)
	at java.base/java.util.ArrayList.add(ArrayList.java:467)
	at com.contoso.risk.engine.MonteCarloSimulator.runSimulation(MonteCarloSimulator.java:342)
	at com.contoso.risk.engine.MonteCarloSimulator.calculateVaR(MonteCarloSimulator.java:128)
	at com.contoso.risk.service.RiskCalculationService.computePortfolioRisk(RiskCalculationService.java:89)
	at com.contoso.risk.service.RiskCalculationService.lambda$processAll$0(RiskCalculationService.java:45)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ReferencePipeline$3$1.accept(ReferencePipeline.java:197)
	at java.base/java.util.ArrayList$ArrayListSpliterator.forEachRemaining(ArrayList.java:1625)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.copyInto(AbstractPipeline.java:509)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.wrapAndCopyInto(AbstractPipeline.java:499)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ReduceOps$ReduceTask.doLeaf(ReduceOps.java:960)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ReduceOps$ReduceTask.doLeaf(ReduceOps.java:934)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractTask.compute(AbstractTask.java:327)
	at java.base/java.util.concurrent.CountedCompleter.exec(CountedCompleter.java:754)
	at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ForkJoinTask.doExec(ForkJoinTask.java:387)
	at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ForkJoinPool$WorkQueue.topLevelExec(ForkJoinPool.java:1312)
	at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ForkJoinPool.scan(ForkJoinPool.java:1843)
	at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ForkJoinPool.runWorker(ForkJoinPool.java:1808)
	at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ForkJoinWorkerThread.run(ForkJoinWorkerThread.java:188)

Heap usage at crash: 7.8GB / 8GB configured. This happens during the daily VaR computation around 6 AM but we didn't notice until traders started reporting stale risk numbers this morning. The service has been restarted but will likely crash again tomorrow at the same time. We need the heap increased or the batch job optimized. About 200 portfolios are affected.

subject: MFA authentication logs show anomaly
description:
While reviewing authentication logs, I found unusual MFA token validation patterns. The base64-encoded log extract is below:



The decoded portion shows MFA token validation failures for sarah.chen@contoso.com with expired tokens. However, the raw log also contains what appears to be binary data appended after the text portion, which shouldn't be there. This suggests the log file may have been tampered with or there's a buffer overflow issue in the logging module.

Additionally, here's a network capture snippet that shows unusual traffic patterns:



I believe this warrants immediate investigation. The failed MFA attempts might indicate a brute-force attack or a compromised authentication module.
